//: redb.Identity.Core module configuration template (Tsak Layer 4: {Module}.config.json).
Override any value via the standard Tsak 5-layer pipeline:
Layer 1 (host appsettings): Tsak:Contexts:default:Identity:*
Layer 2 (per-context):      Tsak:Contexts:{contextName}:Identity:*
Layer 3 (context.json):     same shape as below, merged before this file
Layer 4 (THIS file):        defaults shipped with the .tpkg
Layer 5 (Override, wins):   Tsak:Contexts:{contextName}:Override:Identity:*
Environment-variable overrides use the standard .NET IConfiguration mapping, e.g.:
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__Identity__AccessTokenLifetime=01:00:00
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__Identity__RateLimit__Enabled=true
SECRETS — there is intentionally NO 'SecretsFilePath' option. Do NOT commit real
secrets to this file. All secret-bearing fields are designed to be supplied at
runtime via the Layer-5 Override channel (env-vars, Docker secrets, K8s secrets
mounted as env, or host appsettings outside source control). The fields that
qualify as secrets are:
Identity:RecoveryCodePepper                                   (base64 random)
Identity:DynamicRegistrationInitialAccessToken                (bearer token)
Identity:FederationProviders[i]:ClientSecret                  (per provider)
Identity:RateLimit:RedisConnectionString                      (if Redis backend)
Identity:FederationState:RedisConnectionString                (if Redis backend)
Redb:{instance}:ConnectionString                              (DB credentials)
Example env-var (Linux/Docker, double-underscore = section separator):
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__Identity__RecoveryCodePepper=<base64>
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__Identity__FederationProviders__0__ClientSecret=<value>
Cryptographic keys (SigningCredentials/EncryptionCredentials) are NOT loaded from
JSON. Set AllowEphemeralKeys=true for development. For production they are loaded
from a redb-backed RSA key store (mechanism #2) — see the Identity bootstrap docs.
OBSERVABILITY (F3) — the Identity routes emit OTEL spans + metrics on the security-
critical hot paths. Names are static (cardinality-safe) and surface in any OTEL or
Prometheus exporter the host wires up:
identity.token-request    span + counter + latency histogram (token endpoint)
identity.login            span + counter + latency histogram (credential check)
identity.mfa-verify       span + counter + latency histogram (TOTP / OTP verify)
Plus the per-module health probe registered as 'module:identity' in Tsak's
/api/health/{startup,live,ready} endpoints (database + signing-key + DP-keyring).
Enable Tsak's Prometheus scrape endpoint via host appsettings:
Tsak:Metrics:Prometheus:Enabled=true
Tsak:Metrics:Prometheus:Path=/metrics

FederationProviders.//: Example placeholder — remove or replace with real providers.

//: Secure-by-default: TrustForwardedFor=false ignores X-Forwarded-For entirely
and uses the socket IP as the client IP. Enable ONLY when behind a trusted
proxy whose IPs are listed below — otherwise per-IP rate limits can be spoofed.

RateLimit.//: C1: per-IP and per-(IP+username) throttling on /connect/token and /login.

//: Server-side input-length limits applied by management & registration processors.
Regex overrides: leave null to use the source-generated defaults shown in comments.
NOTE: password length/policy lives in 'PasswordPolicy' below — Min/MaxPasswordLength
here remain only for legacy non-password fields (client_secret length sanity etc.).
H10 — server-side password policy applied by:
• POST   /api/v1/identity/users         (admin Create)
• PUT    /api/v1/identity/users/{id}/password  (admin Change)
• PUT    /me/password                    (self-service)
• POST   /scim/v2/Users  + PATCH         (SCIM)
Defaults are STRICT (NIST SP 800-63B / OWASP ASVS 4.0.3 §2.1).
Each knob is overridable through the standard 5-layer Tsak pipeline, e.g.:
Tsak__Contexts__identity__Override__Identity__PasswordPolicy__MinLength=14
Set HistoryCount=0 to disable history checks entirely.
Set MaxAge='00:00:00' to disable forced password expiration on login.
Set BreachCheckEnabled=true ONLY when a checker is registered (e.g. via the
redb.Identity.PasswordPolicy.Hibp companion package); otherwise the flag is a no-op.
C6 — hardening of the federation state parameter (encrypted with DataProtection).
StateMaxAge: TTL for the encrypted blob (HH:mm:ss). Issued-at timestamp is checked against this.
RequireOneTimeUse: reject replay of the same state blob (jti tracked in the nonce store).
RequireBrowserBinding: tie the state to a per-flow cookie (set on /challenge, verified on /callback).
Backend: 'memory' (default; per-process) or 'redis' (cluster-safe; required behind multiple instances).
RedisConnectionString: optional override; falls back to RateLimit.RedisConnectionString when null.
